title: campanula pusilla subsp. tenella
intro: Campanula pusilla subsp. tenella, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a low-growing, perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The alpine bellflower has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Alpine bellflower can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for potential health benefits.
